basicPlan:
Here’s a balanced, results-driven 6-day workout plan designed to build muscle, increase strength, and improve conditioning while keeping recovery optimal. Follow it consistently for 6–8 weeks.

---

Weekly Split

Day 1 – Push (Chest, Shoulders, Triceps)
Day 2 – Pull (Back, Biceps)
Day 3 – Legs (Strength + Stability)
Day 4 – Conditioning & Core
Day 5 – Upper Hypertrophy
Day 6 – Lower Hypertrophy + Glutes
Day 7 – Rest / Active Recovery

---

Day 1 – Push

- Barbell Bench Press – 4×5–6
- Incline Dumbbell Press – 3×8–10
- Seated Overhead Press – 3×6–8
- Lateral Raises – 4×12–15
- Cable Triceps Pushdowns – 3×10–12
- Overhead Triceps Extension – 2×12–15

---

Day 2 – Pull

- Deadlift or Rack Pull – 4×4–6
- Pull-ups / Lat Pulldown – 4×8–10
- Barbell Row – 3×6–8
- Face Pulls – 3×12–15
- Barbell Curl – 3×8–10
- Hammer Curl – 2×12

---

Day 3 – Legs (Strength)

- Back Squat – 5×3–5
- Romanian Deadlift – 3×6–8
- Walking Lunges – 3×10 each leg
- Leg Curl – 3×10–12
- Standing Calf Raise – 4×12–15

---

Day 4 – Conditioning & Core

- Sled Push / Bike Sprints – 10×20 sec ON / 40 sec OFF
- Hanging Leg Raises – 4×12
- Ab Wheel Rollouts – 3×10
- Plank Variations – 3×45–60 sec
- Mobility work (hips, spine, shoulders)

---

Day 5 – Upper Hypertrophy

- Incline Bench Press – 4×8–10
- Chest-Supported Dumbbell Rows – 4×10–12
- Cable Flyes – 3×12–15
- Lateral Raise Dropset – 3 rounds
- EZ-Bar Curl – 3×12
- Rope Triceps Extensions – 3×12–15

---

Day 6 – Lower Hypertrophy & Glutes

- Front Squat or Hack Squat – 4×8–10
- Hip Thrusts – 4×10–12
- Bulgarian Split Squats – 3×10 each leg
- Leg Extension – 3×15
- Seated Calf Raise – 4×15–20

---

Training Rules

- Progressive overload every week (more weight or reps)
- Rest: 2–3 min on compounds, 60–90 sec on accessories
- Tempo: Controlled negatives, explosive positives
- Warm-up before every session, stretch after

---

Expected Results (with proper nutrition)

- Increased muscle density and strength
- Noticeable fat loss and conditioning
- Improved posture and joint stability

Train with intent. Recover hard. Stay consistent.
threeDaysPlan:
Here’s a balanced, effective 3-day gym workout plan designed for a healthy 25-year-old. It builds strength, muscle, and general fitness while allowing proper recovery between sessions.

---

Weekly Structure (3 Days)

Day 1 – Push (Chest, Shoulders, Triceps)
Day 2 – Pull (Back, Biceps)
Day 3 – Legs & Core

Train on non-consecutive days (e.g. Mon / Wed / Fri).

---

Day 1 – Push
Focus: upper-body pushing strength and shoulder stability.

1. Barbell or Dumbbell Bench Press – 4 × 6–8
2. Incline Dumbbell Press – 3 × 8–10
3. Seated Shoulder Press – 3 × 8–10
4. Lateral Raises – 3 × 12–15
5. Cable or Machine Chest Fly – 3 × 10–12
6. Triceps Pushdowns – 3 × 10–12
7. Overhead Triceps Extension – 2 × 12–15

Rest: 60–90 sec
Finish: Light stretch for chest & shoulders

---

Day 2 – Pull
Focus: back thickness, posture, and arm strength.

1. Deadlift or Romanian Deadlift – 4 × 5–6
2. Pull-Ups or Lat Pulldown – 3 × 8–10
3. Barbell or Dumbbell Rows – 3 × 8–10
4. Seated Cable Row – 3 × 10–12
5. Face Pulls – 3 × 12–15
6. Barbell or EZ-Bar Curls – 3 × 8–10
7. Hammer Curls – 2 × 10–12

Rest: 60–120 sec
Finish: Stretch lats & biceps

---

Day 3 – Legs & Core
Focus: full lower-body strength and trunk stability.

1. Squats (Back or Front) – 4 × 6–8
2. Leg Press – 3 × 10
3. Walking Lunges – 3 × 10/leg
4. Romanian Deadlift or Leg Curl – 3 × 10–12
5. Standing or Seated Calf Raises – 4 × 12–15

Core Circuit (3 rounds):
- Hanging Leg Raises – 10–12
- Plank – 45–60 sec
- Cable Crunch – 12–15

---

Optional Cardio:
Add 10–20 minutes after workouts or on rest days (incline walking, cycling, rowing, stair machine).

Progression Rules:
Increase weight once you hit the top of the rep range with good form. Prioritize controlled reps and full range of motion. Deload every 6–8 weeks if needed.

Recovery Basics:
Sleep 7–9 hours, stay hydrated, warm up before lifting, stretch tight muscles after.

This plan is structured, efficient, and sustainable—perfect for consistent progress with a 3-day schedule. Stay disciplined and results will follow.
musclePlan:
Here is a muscle-focused training plan built to maximize hypertrophy, strength, and recovery for a healthy 25-year-old. Follow this consistently for 8–10 weeks.

---

Training Split (5 Days / Week)

Day 1 – Push (Chest, Shoulders, Triceps)
Day 2 – Pull (Back, Biceps)
Day 3 – Legs (Quads, Hamstrings, Glutes)
Day 4 – Upper Hypertrophy
Day 5 – Lower Hypertrophy + Core

---

Day 1 – Push

- Barbell Bench Press – 4×6–8
- Incline Dumbbell Press – 3×8–10
- Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press – 3×8–10
- Lateral Raises – 4×12–15
- Cable Triceps Pushdowns – 3×10–12
- Overhead Triceps Extensions – 2×12–15

---

Day 2 – Pull

- Deadlift or Rack Pull – 4×4–6
- Pull-ups or Lat Pulldown – 4×8–10
- Barbell Row – 3×6–8
- Face Pulls – 3×12–15
- Barbell Curl – 3×8–10
- Incline Dumbbell Curl – 2×12

---

Day 3 – Legs

- Back Squat – 4×5–6
- Romanian Deadlift – 3×6–8
- Leg Press – 3×10
- Lying Leg Curl – 3×10–12
- Standing Calf Raises – 4×12–15

---

Day 4 – Upper Hypertrophy

- Incline Bench Press – 4×8–10
- Chest-Supported Dumbbell Rows – 4×10–12
- Cable Flyes – 3×12–15
- Lateral Raise Dropsets – 3 rounds
- EZ-Bar Curl – 3×12
- Rope Triceps Extensions – 3×12–15

---

Day 5 – Lower Hypertrophy + Core

- Front Squat or Hack Squat – 4×8–10
- Hip Thrusts – 4×10–12
- Bulgarian Split Squats – 3×10 each leg
- Leg Extensions – 3×15
- Hanging Leg Raises – 3×12
- Plank – 3×60 sec

---

Muscle-Building Rules

- Progressive overload every week
- Rest: 2–3 min on heavy lifts, 60–90 sec on accessories
- Tempo: slow controlled lowering, explosive lifting
- Volume target: 10–20 sets per muscle group per week

---

Recovery & Nutrition Essentials

- Sleep 7–9 hours nightly
- Eat in a caloric surplus with high protein intake
- Deload every 8–10 weeks if strength stalls

---

Expected Results

- Noticeable muscle growth across chest, back, legs, and shoulders
- Increased strength and training capacity
- Fuller, more athletic physique

Train hard, eat to grow, recover aggressively.
threeDaysMusclePlan:
Here’s a professional, muscle-building workout plan designed for 3 gym days per week. It’s balanced, progressive, and ideal for a healthy 25-year-old aiming to gain lean muscle.

---

Training Principles (Follow These Strictly)

Frequency: 3 days/week (e.g., Mon–Wed–Fri)
Rest: 60–90 sec for hypertrophy, 2–3 min for big lifts
Progressive overload: Add weight or reps weekly
Tempo: Controlled (2 sec lift / 3 sec lower)
Warm-up: 5–10 min + light sets before compounds

---

Day 1 – Push (Chest, Shoulders, Triceps)
Goal: Upper-body pressing strength & size

1. Barbell Bench Press – 4×6–8
2. Incline Dumbbell Press – 3×8–10
3. Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press – 3×8–10
4. Lateral Raises – 3×12–15
5. Cable Triceps Pushdowns – 3×10–12
6. Overhead Triceps Extension – 2×12–15

Finisher (optional): Push-ups to failure ×2

---

Day 2 – Pull (Back, Biceps, Rear Delts)
Goal: Thickness, width, and arm development

1. Deadlift or Romanian Deadlift – 3×5–6
2. Pull-ups or Lat Pulldowns – 4×8–10
3. Barbell or Chest-Supported Rows – 3×8–10
4. Face Pulls – 3×12–15
5. Barbell or EZ-Bar Curls – 3×8–10
6. Incline Dumbbell Curls – 2×10–12

---

Day 3 – Legs & Core
Goal: Full lower-body mass & stability

1. Back Squat – 4×6–8
2. Leg Press – 3×10–12
3. Romanian Deadlift – 3×8–10
4. Walking Lunges – 3×12/leg
5. Standing Calf Raises – 4×12–15
6. Hanging Leg Raises – 3×10–15
7. Plank – 3×45–60 sec

---

Weekly Progression
Weeks 1–4: Focus on form, steady increases
Week 5: Deload (reduce volume by 30–40%)
Weeks 6–10: Push intensity harder

---

Nutrition Guidelines (Non-Negotiable)
Protein: ~1.6–2.2 g/kg bodyweight daily
Calories: Slight surplus (+250–400 kcal)
Hydration: ≥2.5 L/day
Post-workout: Protein + carbs within 60 min

---

Recovery
Sleep 7–9 hours
Light cardio or mobility on rest days
Stretch trained muscles post-session

Stick to this plan consistently for 8–10 weeks and you will gain measurable muscle, strength, and definition.
